item.title: Italy’s Ludoil agrees to buy Beny Steinmetz-linked Sicilian oil refinery
item.description: Russia’s Lukoil sold the ISAB plant in 2022 to a Cypriot company backed by the convicted Israeli tycoon

item.title: Brown-Forman’s $17bn bid rejection leaves a bitter taste
item.description: The Brown family may have balked at handing its brands over to Sazerac, but it would have been more appealing than the alternatives

item.title: New York cuts transport prices to World Cup matches it is hosting
item.description: Round-trip bus ticket from New York City to MetLife Stadium reduced from $80 to $20

item.title: Eric Trump joins Beijing trip as family-linked group chases China deal
item.description: Company with ties to US president’s son has MOU with chipmaker that Congress warned is connected to Communist Party

item.title: Crispin Odey settles sexual assault claims
item.description: Financier had been sued for personal injury by women

item.title: Ex-SEC chair Clayton says he does ‘not see excess leverage’ in private credit
item.description: Wall Street’s top prosecutor says the industry helped the US recover faster than Europe from the 2008 crisis

item.title: White-collar workers report growing feelings of ‘AI brain fry’
item.description: Workers are reporting feeling overwhelmed by the new technology

item.title: Boots to appoint outgoing Currys boss Baldock as CEO
item.description: UK pharmacy chain finalising appointment of Alex Baldock as it prepares for potential London listing next year

item.title: JPMorgan reshuffles investment bank chiefs amid wider shake-up
item.description: Dorothee Blessing, Kevin Foley and Jared Kaye appointed co-heads of global investment banking

item.title: Czech arms group makes offer for stake in tank maker KNDS
item.description: CSG’s proposal to Franco-German company marks new attempt to consolidate European defence industry

item.title: How the dream of a non-profit OpenAI died
item.description: What’s really behind the Musk-Altman legal battle?

item.title: The rise of the American corporate gerontocracy
item.description: No country for young CEOs

item.title: EU to force rail operators to sell rival services
item.description: Brussels seeks to replicate air travel bookings for trains, ferries and coaches

item.title: FirstFT: JPMorgan reshuffles its top bankers
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Sam Altman takes the stand in the OpenAI trial and Trump flies to Beijing

item.title: Food companies hit by Trump administration’s cuts to benefits
item.description: US consumer demand drops after households lose access to federal Snap subsidy programme

item.title: Why scrapping quarterly earnings is a bad idea
item.description: US chief executives should be wary of putting their own convenience ahead of transparency

item.title: Vistry’s shares plunge as discounted house sales erode profit
item.description: UK housebuilder warns first-half profits will drop significantly as it slashes home prices to generate cash

item.title: GameStop makes big bet that governance doesn’t matter
item.description: With his offer rebuffed by eBay, the ball is now in GameStop boss Ryan Cohen’s court

item.title: BYD says ‘flash charging’ tech will power market share gains as it takes on Europe
item.description: Chinese group’s technology allows electric vehicles to be charged in as little as five minutes

item.title: SoftBank profits surge on $25bn gain for OpenAI stake
item.description: Japanese group books net income of $11.6bn in fourth quarter, vastly ahead of analyst expectations

item.title: IEA warns of further price spikes as global oil inventories fall at record pace
item.description: Iran war has cut into oil consumption but not enough to halt ‘rapid’ draw on inventories

item.title: Intertek set to agree £10.6bn takeover by private equity group EQT
item.description: Sweden-based buyout firm made ‘final’ offer of £60 a share for UK company

item.title: Defence tech start-up Anduril doubles valuation to over $60bn
item.description: Peter Thiel-backed company raises $5bn as the US administration turns to smaller players for latest military kit

item.title: Bank stocks under pressure after Australia targets property investors
item.description: Commonwealth Bank slides 10 per cent amid budget changes and darkening economic outlook

item.title: Miami got NYC’s billionaires. Will it get their businesses?
item.description: Wall Street South vs Wall Street North

item.title: Tech Exchange
item.description: This series features conversations between FT journalists and the technology industry’s leaders, innovators, and thinkers. In the latest instalment, Amazon’s Panos Panay, the head of the group’s device unit talks about the hardware push that follows Alexa+

item.title: Introducing: The Story of Money
item.description: A new video podcast from The FT exploring the history of finance

item.title: BuzzFeed and GoPro learn nostalgia doesn’t pay the bills
item.description: The goodwill of middle-aged consumers won’t be enough to generate a meaningful return

item.title: The takeover that’s the talk of London
item.description: The £10.6bn showdown between EQT and Intertek is pitting top UK rainmakers against each other

item.title: UK veterinary group CVS targeted by activist investor
item.description: Converium Capital complains vet company’s stock has not recovered after UK regulators finished an industry probe
